text: However, recent literature confirmed that despite its potent intracellular presence exerting cytoprotective effects, Prdx1 would transform into a novel damage-associated molecular pattern (DAMP) when abnormally released into the extracellular space due to pathological factors such as pancreatic necrosis and then actively bound to Toll-like receptors (TLRs) on immune cells, exacerbating pro-inflammatory immune responses and worsening tissue damage[16–18]. Our previous studies have also demonstrated that in the early stages of SAP, Prdx1 expression within acinar cells increased as a compensatory antioxidant response. However, as the disease progresses to SAP, intracellular PDRX1 levels were insufficient to resist explosive ROS, leading to increased oxidative stress and cellular dysfunction. Concurrently, Prdx1 protein exhibited high-level extracellular accumulation in various inflammatory diseases, further fueling destructive inflammatory cascades. Therefore, the key question is how to simultaneously supplement and enhance intracellular Prdx1 while precisely neutralizing extracellular Prdx1 to exert anti-inflammatory effects.
